author: jdrowne
text: VERR_ACCESS_DENIED when using 'createhd' with Vagrant and Virtualbox

author: jdrowne
text:
# Issue Type

* Bug report

# Molecule and Ansible details

```
ansible 2.8.5
  config file = None
  configured module search path = ['/home/juliett/.ansible/plugins/modules', '/usr/share/ansible/plugins/modules']
  ansible python module location = /usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/ansible
  executable location = /usr/local/bin/ansible

molecule, version 2.22
```

Molecule installation method (one of):

* pip

Ansible installation method (one of):

* pip

# Desired Behavior

Molecule creates an instance with an additional hard drive based on:

molecule.yml:

```
driver:
  name: vagrant
  provider:
    name: virtualbox
lint:
  name: yamllint
platforms:
  - name: ubuntu1604
    box: ubuntu/xenial64
    memory: 2048
    cpus: 2
    provider_raw_config_args:
      - "customize [ 'createhd', '--filename', './tmpDisk.vdi', '--variant', 'Fixed', '--size', 20 * 1024 ]"
      - "customize [ 'storageattach', :id, '--storagectl', 'SATAController', '--port', 1, '--device', 0, '--type', 'hdd', '--medium', './tmpDisk.vdi' ]"
```

author: jdrowne
text:
Following Vagrantfile works fine on the same machine:

```
disk = './secondDisk.vdi'
Vagrant.configure(2) do |config|
  config.vm.define "iscsitarget" do |iscsitarget|
    iscsitarget.vm.box = "ubuntu/trusty64"
    iscsitarget.vm.hostname = "iscsitarget"

    iscsitarget.vm.provider "virtualbox" do |vb|
      unless File.exist?(disk)
        vb.customize ['createhd', '--filename', disk, '--variant', 'Fixed', '--size', 20 * 1024]
      end
      vb.memory = "1024"
      vb.customize ['storageattach', :id,  '--storagectl', 'SATAController', '--port', 1, '--device', 0, '--type', 'hdd', '--medium', disk]
    end
  end
end
```

author: jdrowne
text: [@decentral1se](https://github.com/decentral1se) I tried adding `customize [ 'modifyvm', :id, '--uartmode1', 'disconnected' ]` to my `molecule.yml` and to `/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/molecule/driver/vagrant.py` but I get the same error. But yes, it seems that Molecule is trying to create `tmpDisk.vdi` within `/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/molecule/provisioner/ansible/playbooks/vagrant/` rather than within the `$TMPDIR`.

author: elisdg
text:
EDIT2:  
... changing the path in the provider\_raw\_config\_args-customization to reflect the `{{ item }}`, the code below is now working...

---

I struggled with the same behavior in molecule.  
But after getting this also in kitchenci, I discovered: [hashicorp/vagrant#8107](https://github.com/hashicorp/vagrant/issues/8107)  
**Problem**: molecule doesnt "close" the disks when it fails, and also it doesnt close it on "destroying"  
Look for the molecule-machine in `vboxmanage list hdds`

The **workaround** is (at least in my case) to delete the disks in the VirtualBox "Virtual Media Manager", or:

```
vboxmanage list vms   |perl -laE'`vboxmanage unregistervm --delete $F[1]`'       # remove ALL VMs
vboxmanage list hdds  |perl -laE'`vboxmanage closemedium disk --delete $F[1]`    # remove ALL disks
```

After this I was able to run `molecule converge` whith the following "create.yml"-file:

```
---
- hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  tasks:
    - name: Create instances
      molecule_vagrant:
        instance_name: "{{ item }}"
        platform_box: debian/stretch64
        provider_name: virtualbox
        provider_memory: 768
        provider_cpus: 1
        provider_raw_config_args:
          - "customize [ 'modifyvm', :id, '--uartmode1', 'disconnected' ]"
          - "customize [ 'createhd', '--filename', '{{ item }}/sdb.vmdk', '--format', 'vmdk', '--size', '30000' ]"
          - "customize [ 'storagectl', :id, '--name', 'SATA Controller', '--portcount', '4']"
          - "customize [ 'storageattach', :id, '--storagectl', 'SATA Controller', '--port', '4', '--device', '0', '--type', 'hdd', '--medium', '{{ item }}/sdb.vmdk']"
        provision: true
        state: up
      with_items:
        - demo-1
        - demo-2
```
